revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Ruiju Kokushi]][[en:Ruijū Kokushi]][[ff:Ruijū Kokushi]][[fr:Ruijū kokushi]][[ja:類聚国史]][[ko:유취국사]][[zh:類聚國史]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1038639]] Wikidata name: Ruijū Kokushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Ruijū Kokushi'''''|類聚国史||}} is a historical text that categorizes and chronologizes the events listed in the ''[[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]''. It was compiled by [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] and completed in 892. The text was commissioned by [[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]].
==Contents==
The text was originally 200 volumes in length with two indices and three genealogical volumes. However, much of it was lost during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]] in the late 15th century. Only 62 volumes are currently extant: volumes 1-5, 8-11, 14-16, 19, 25, 28, 31-36, 40, 54, 61, 66, 71-75, 77-80, 83-84, 86-89, 99, 101, 107, 147, 159, 165, 170-171, 173, 177-180, 182, 185-187, 189-190, 193-194, 199.<ref name="Bungaku Daijiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten (1986:1938)</ref>
The text categories and subcategories the historical accounts given in the ''Six National Histories'', listing each of which in chronological order..<ref name="Koten Bungaku Jiten">Koten Bungaku Jiten (1997:1507)</ref> There are a total of eighteen categories, with another five lost in the missing volumes.
While the final national history, ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', was completed in 901, nine years later, Michizane was one of the contributing editors and was thus able to incorporate early content.<ref name="Bungaku Daijiten" />
The editorial policy was to be true to the original sources without modifying or adding to the content. It is thus a valuable resource in reconstructing ''[[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]]'', ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'', and ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', all of which were largely lost over time. <ref name="Bungaku Daijiten" />
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * {{NKBJ}} * {{NKBD}}
{{Japan-hist-stub}}
{{Rikkokushi}}
{{draft categories|
[[Category:Late Old Japanese texts]] [[Category:9th-century history books]] [[Category:History books about Japan]] [[Category:Nara period]] [[Category:892]] [[Category:9th century in Japan]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:History books of the Heian Period]]
}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Ruiju Kokushi]][[en:Ruijū Kokushi]][[ff:Ruijū Kokushi]][[fr:Ruijū kokushi]][[ja:類聚国史]][[ko:유취국사]][[zh:類聚國史]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Ruiju Kokushi]][[en:Ruijū Kokushi]][[ff:Ruijū Kokushi]][[fr:Ruijū kokushi]][[ja:類聚国史]][[ko:유취국사]][[zh:類聚國史]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ruiju Kokushi]][[en:Ruijū Kokushi]][[ff:Ruijū Kokushi]][[fr:Ruijū kokushi]][[ja:類聚国史]][[ko:유취국사]][[zh:類聚國史]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1038639]] Wikidata name: Ruijū Kokushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Ruijū Kokushi'''''|類聚国史||}} is a historical text that categorizes and chronologizes the events listed in the ''[[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]''. It was compiled by [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] and completed in 892. The text was commissioned by [[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]].
==Contents==
The text was originally 200 volumes in length with two indices and three genealogical volumes. However, much of it was lost during the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]] in the late 15th century. Only 62 volumes are currently extant: volumes 1-5, 8-11, 14-16, 19, 25, 28, 31-36, 40, 54, 61, 66, 71-75, 77-80, 83-84, 86-89, 99, 101, 107, 147, 159, 165, 170-171, 173, 177-180, 182, 185-187, 189-190, 193-194, 199.<ref name="Bungaku Daijiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten (1986:1938)</ref>
The text categories and subcategories the historical accounts given in the ''Six National Histories'', listing each of which in chronological order..<ref name="Koten Bungaku Jiten">Koten Bungaku Jiten (1997:1507)</ref> There are a total of eighteen categories, with another five lost in the missing volumes.
While the final national history, ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', was completed in 901, nine years later, Michizane was one of the contributing editors and was thus able to incorporate early content.<ref name="Bungaku Daijiten" />
The editorial policy was to be true to the original sources without modifying or adding to the content. It is thus a valuable resource in reconstructing ''[[:en:Nihon Kōki|Nihon Kōki]]'', ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'', and ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', all of which were largely lost over time. <ref name="Bungaku Daijiten" />
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * {{NKBJ}} * {{NKBD}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ruiju Kokushi]][[en:Ruijū Kokushi]][[ff:Ruijū Kokushi]][[fr:Ruijū kokushi]][[ja:類聚国史]][[ko:유취국사]][[zh:類聚國史]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ruiju Kokushi]][[en:Ruijū Kokushi]][[ff:Ruijū Kokushi]][[fr:Ruijū kokushi]][[ja:類聚国史]][[ko:유취국사]][[zh:類聚國史]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ruiju Kokushi]][[en:Ruijū Kokushi]][[ff:Ruijū Kokushi]][[fr:Ruijū kokushi]][[ja:類聚国史]][[ko:유취국사]][[zh:類聚國史]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1038639]] Wikidata name: Ruijū Kokushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Ruijū Kokushi'''''|類聚国史||}} is a historical text that categorizes and chronologizes the events listed in the ''[[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]''. It was compiled by {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} and completed in 892. The text was commissioned by {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda}}.
==Contents==
The text was originally 200 volumes in length with two indices and three genealogical volumes. However, much of it was lost during the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|WD=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War}} in the late 15th century. Only 62 volumes are currently extant: volumes 1-5, 8-11, 14-16, 19, 25, 28, 31-36, 40, 54, 61, 66, 71-75, 77-80, 83-84, 86-89, 99, 101, 107, 147, 159, 165, 170-171, 173, 177-180, 182, 185-187, 189-190, 193-194, 199.<ref name="Bungaku Daijiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten (1986:1938)</ref>
The text categories and subcategories the historical accounts given in the ''Six National Histories'', listing each of which in chronological order..<ref name="Koten Bungaku Jiten">Koten Bungaku Jiten (1997:1507)</ref> There are a total of eighteen categories, with another five lost in the missing volumes.
While the final national history, ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', was completed in 901, nine years later, Michizane was one of the contributing editors and was thus able to incorporate early content.<ref name="Bungaku Daijiten" />
The editorial policy was to be true to the original sources without modifying or adding to the content. It is thus a valuable resource in reconstructing ''{{ill|Nihon Kōki|en|Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1077029|lt=Nihon Kōki}}'', ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'', and ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', all of which were largely lost over time. <ref name="Bungaku Daijiten" />
==Notes== {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ruiju Kokushi]][[en:Ruijū Kokushi]][[ff:Ruijū Kokushi]][[fr:Ruijū kokushi]][[ja:類聚国史]][[ko:유취국사]][[zh:類聚國史]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ruiju Kokushi]][[en:Ruijū Kokushi]][[ff:Ruijū Kokushi]][[fr:Ruijū kokushi]][[ja:類聚国史]][[ko:유취국사]][[zh:類聚國史]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Ruiju Kokushi]][[en:Ruijū Kokushi]][[ff:Ruijū Kokushi]][[fr:Ruijū kokushi]][[ja:類聚国史]][[ko:유취국사]][[zh:類聚國史]]
{{italic title}} {{Nihongo|'''''Ruijū Kokushi'''''|類聚国史||}} is a historical text that categorizes and chronologizes the events listed in the ''[[Rikkokushi|Six National Histories]]''. It was compiled by {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} and completed in 892. The text was commissioned by {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda|1=Emperor Uda}}.
==Contents==
The text was originally 200 volumes in length with two indices and three genealogical volumes. However, much of it was lost during the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|WD=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War|1=Ōnin War}} in the late 15th century. Only 62 volumes are currently extant: volumes 1-5, 8-11, 14-16, 19, 25, 28, 31-36, 40, 54, 61, 66, 71-75, 77-80, 83-84, 86-89, 99, 101, 107, 147, 159, 165, 170-171, 173, 177-180, 182, 185-187, 189-190, 193-194, 199.<ref name="Bungaku Daijiten">Nihon Koten Bungaku Daijiten (1986:1938)</ref>
The text categories and subcategories the historical accounts given in the ''Six National Histories'', listing each of which in chronological order..<ref name="Koten Bungaku Jiten">Koten Bungaku Jiten (1997:1507)</ref> There are a total of eighteen categories, with another five lost in the missing volumes.
While the final national history, ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', was completed in 901, nine years later, Michizane was one of the contributing editors and was thus able to incorporate early content.<ref name="Bungaku Daijiten" />
The editorial policy was to be true to the original sources without modifying or adding to the content. It is thus a valuable resource in reconstructing ''{{ill|Nihon Kōki|en|Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1077029|lt=Nihon Kōki|1=Nihon Kōki}}'', ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'', and ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', all of which were largely lost over time. <ref name="Bungaku Daijiten" />

The text categories and subcategories the historical accounts given in the ''Six National Histories'', listing each of which in chronological order..<ref name="Koten Bungaku Jiten">Koten Bungaku Jiten (1997:1507)</ref> There are a total of eighteen categories, with another five lost in the missing volumes.
While the final national history, ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', was completed in 901, nine years later, Michizane was one of the contributing editors and was thus able to incorporate early content.<ref name="Bungaku Daijiten" />

It is thus a valuable resource in reconstructing ''{{ill|Nihon Kōki|en|Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1077029|lt=Nihon Kōki|1=Nihon Kōki}}'', ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]'', and ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', all of which were largely lost over time. <ref name="Bungaku Daijiten" />
